limatours_text: Lima Tours, since its foundation in 1956, has followed the motto of  "Designing magnificent experiences in Peru, tailored to each guest ". We pride ourselves in offering innovative and memorable experiences, meticulously taking care of every detail when designing and managing our passengers' trips. Our proposals range from traditional tours to adrenaline-filled adventures and excitement. In addition, we stand out as the first Carbon Neutral Tour Operator in Peru, certified by Green Initiative, establishing leading environmental standards in the country.
foodsText: Daily breakfast at the hotel, lunches and dinners as specified in the itinerary.

terminos_y_condiciones_punto_1: All rates are per person and apply to foreign passengers only.
terminos_y_condiciones_punto_2: Prices are subject to change without notice and can only be guaranteed after booking.
terminos_y_condiciones_punto_3: Rates do not apply to residents with a stay longer than 60 days or to corporate passengers (from 4 nights or more in Lima).
terminos_y_condiciones_punto_4: Reservations must include each passenger’s full name, nationality, passport number, age and date of birth.
terminos_y_condiciones_punto_5: If the suggested/listed hotels for this program are not available at the time of confirmation, LimaTours will offer new hotel alternatives with revised prices.
terminos_y_condiciones_punto_6: Early arrival on day 1 does not guarantee the availability of hotel rooms. Standard check-in times apply, unless additional nights of accommodations are purchased to ensure immediate access to rooms.
terminos_y_condiciones_punto_7: Our accommodation rates include breakfast. Breakfasts have assigned schedules according to the policies of each hotel establishment, being generally between 06:00 – 10:00; if the passenger has a transfer or withdraw before the assigned time, the passenger will lose this benefit, not being able to be compensated in another hotel. The services of Room Service or of a box breakfast have an additional cost and don’t compensate the breakfast that can’t be served.
terminos_y_condiciones_punto_8: For reservations with train services to Machu Picchu, the seats are assigned according to the availability of the car. Locations can only be arranged after the ticket has been issued.
Recommendation_line1: Comfortable and light clothes are the most convenient choice for traveling on Peru’s coast, while warmer clothes are best for stays in the highlands. Passengers must not wear sleeveless T-shirts, shorts, miniskirts or very tight clothes when visiting temples, religious places or official buildings. Sunblock lotion, sun glasses, a hat or cap and a mosquito repellent can be also very helpful during the trip.
Recommendation_line2: Please have in mind that check-in hour in most hotels starts at 13:00 hrs. Early-arriving guests will be subject to room availability, while waiting in the hotel lobby.

titlepolicyInca: TERMS AND CONDITIONS FOR INCA TRAIL (ONLY IF INCA TRAIL SERVICE IS APPLIED)
policyInca_line1: According to the current regulations (Resolution N° 02-2003 - UGM- CD ), access to the 4 day Inca Trail is limited to 500 people per day and sold out months in advance. Between April and October, tickets are usually exhausted with 4 or 5 months in advance.
policyInca_line2: Once the Inca Trail entry is booked, you cannot cancel, change the date or the name of the person who will trek it, as the institution that regulates the Inca Trail (INC) does not allow any change and does not return the money spent on entries.
subtitle_policyInca: IMPORTANT INFORMARTION TO ENSURE THE PURCHASE OF INCA TRAIL ENTRANCE TICKET:
policyInca_data2: PASSPORT NUMBER (Valid for Inca trail) or Identification Number
policyInca_data5: Indicate you have physical conditions for adventure hiking

HuaynaPicchu_data1: Those who trek the Inca Trail cannot access the entrance to Huayna Picchu. A new entrance ticket to Machu Picchu with Huayna Picchu or mountain of Machu Picchu access must be purchased.

textdomation3: (*) Wallata is the Quechua name for the Andean Goose, an endemic bird which inspired a traditional courtship dance, now a ritual in Ollantaytambo feasts.
textImportant_data1: The first entrance to the citadel requires the mandatory accompaniment of a guide. Please note that the guided tour lasts approximately 2 hours and 30 minutes.
textImportant_data2: The entrance to Machu Picchu allows a maximum stay of 4 hours in the citadel, if you want to stay the whole time inform your guide at the beginning of the tour.
textImportant_data3: The route to visit the Machu Picchu citadel and schedules are subject to availability.
textImportant_data4: For shared tours, the visit to the Inca Bridge is not included.
textImportant_data5: For the purchase of tickets to Machu Picchu it is necessary to send full name, date of birth, nationality, passport number of passengers, which must match the current passport that will be used for the trip. It is important to take into account that the entrance tickets to the citadel are non-refundable from the time of purchase.
textImportant_data6: In the case of an overnight stay in the Sacred Valley during shared services, LimaTours will be responsible for transferring only one piece of hand luggage per passenger to the hotel in the Sacred Valley. If the passenger wishes to have the rest of their luggage transferred, an additional fee will apply. In the case of an overnight stay in Cusco, the hand luggage will be stored in the hotel’s luggage room.
textImportant_data7: Carry the same document that you presented to the agency, given that train tickets and entrance to Machu Picchu will be issued with this information.
textImportant_data8: In all train services to Machu Picchu, the passenger may carry only hand luggage (backpack, bag or briefcase) of no more than 8 kg/ 17.6 lb on the train to/from Machu Picchu. You will find your luggage at the chosen hotel.

textGeneralImportant: We recommend comfortable and light clothing for visiting the coastal areas of Peru and warm clothes for the high Andean area. Visitors are not advised to wear sleeves shirts, shorts, short skirts or short clothing when visiting religious temples or official buildings. Sunscreen, sunglasses, hat and mosquito repellent can be very useful during your stay.

textCancelation_text_1: For packages cancellation fees will be charged as follows:
textCancelation_text_2: *Policy applicable for hotels from Comfort to Standard categories. For cancellation policies of Luxury category hotels, consult with your executive.
textCancelation_covid_text: Cancellation charges will not apply in cases where the passengers have been prevented from travelling due to a positive result of COVID-19 days before their trip, presenting the positive result certificate prior to the departure from the country of origin.
textCancelation_line1b: (transfers / tours), charges for cancellation will be applied in the following manner:
textCancelation_list_0: The entrance to Machu Picchu or any other ticket purchase outside this time range, is not considered within these cancellation fees. Once it is issued, a 100% cost of the ticket will be charged.
textCancelation_list_1: These package policies apply for Standard to First Superior categories.
textCancelation_list_2: For Deluxe categories, policies will be sent by your reservation specialist.
textCancelation_list_3: Inca Trail packages, Machu Picchu Citadel entrance, Machu Picchu Mountain and Huayna Picchu entrance, Amazonian cruises, trains and other special services are subject to cancellation charges indicated by your reservation specialist at the time of confirmation.
textCancelation_list_5: In case of no-shows (defined according to hotel policy), there will be a surcharge of 18% (IGV - General Sales Tax) in addition to the rate.
textCancelation_list_6: Groups with over 17 passengers or Incentive Groups may have special cancellation policies depending to the hotels used.
textCancelation_list_7: For MICE groups, different policies apply depending on the hotel, and the client must be informed in the quotation.
textCancelation_list_4: The tickets to Machu Picchu will be purchased as soon as we have the passenger’s information. Once purchased, in case of cancellation, the 100% cost of the ticket will be charged.
textCancelation_line2b: the following cancellation charges will be applied:
textCancelation_line2_sub1: Hotels' policies are subject to change without previous notice.
textCancelation_line2_sub1a: 45 to 1 day: 100% of cancellation fees in the following properties: Belmond Sanctuary Lodge and Inkaterra properties.
textCancelation_line2_sub1b: 31 ro 1 day: 100% of cancellation fees for the rest of the hotels.
textCancelation_line2_sub2: For groups (10 guests or more), cancellation costs will be confirmed by your assigned account executive after the reservation has been completed, including rooming lists and reconfirmation deadline.
textCancelation_line2_sub3: In the event of no-shows (as defined per hotel policy), there will be a surcharge of 18% (VAT – value added tax) on top of the rate.
textCancelation_line2_sub4: Last minute reservations (within 15 days) are considered reconfirmed. There will be a 24 hours window to cancel with no charges.
textCancelation_line2_sub5: The cancellation charges for special services such as all-inclusive Amazon cruises / lodges are based on the policies of each supplier, which you will receive from your assigned account executive after completing your reservation.
textCancelation_line2_sub6: The Inca Trail packages are subject to the cancellation charges indicated by your executive at the time of confirmation.
titleinfoImportant: INFORMATION ABOUT THE ENTRANCE IN PERU
textinfoImportant: Confirm with your embassy if you need a VISA to enter Peru (or any other country included in your itinerary). LimaTours is not responsible if a guest does not have all the necessary documentation to enter the country. The foreigner who travels to Peru must ensure that the validity of his passport is not less than six months to enter the national territory.
titleCaminoIncaInfoInportant: IMPORTANT IMFORMATION ABOUT LUGGAGE IN THE INCA TRAIL
textCaminoInca_privado_text: during the briefing, we will provide to you a duffle bag for your personal belongings. The porters will carry this bag (the maximum weight should not exceed 5 kg / 11lb).
textCaminoInca_text: * The duffle bags must be returned at the end of the trek.
textCaminoInca_compartido_text: do not include the supply of the duffle bag. It is recommended to bring a bag or backpack to carry your belongings. If you do not have your own bag, you can obtain a duffle bag at an additional cost. Also, you can request the service of a porter at an additional cost.
text_lugares_de_reabastecimiento_1: As in many countries, plastic contamination is increasing, an issue that that doesn’t escape from Peru’s wonders. It is not allowed anymore to bring plastic water bottles into over 76 protected natural areas, which include for example Machu Picchu, Paracas reserve or Lake Titicaca!
subtitle_lugares_de_reabastecimiento_1: If you have a reusable bottle, do not hesitate to bring it!
text_lugares_de_reabastecimiento_2: Do to the fact that it is not recommended to drink tap water, we put at your disposal refill points in hotels and other establishments. Along the route, don't hesitate to ask your guide and hotel reception where you can refill your bottle. In the case of multi-day treks, boiled water is used to refill your water bottle everyday!
subtitle_lugares_de_reabastecimiento_2: Some recharging points previously coordinated with our suppliers are:
text_lugares_de_reabastecimiento_3: For road trips of more than 4 hours for our private and shared services, there will be water containers on board to provide the refill service.

masi_parrafo_1: By providing us with your passengers’ email and telephone number with access to WhatsApp, they will receive informative communications about their journey. This includes:
masi_texto_1: A message 7 days prior to arrival with: LimaTours presentation, travel recommendations, map and general information about their trip (only by email).
masi_texto_2: The day before the trip, they will receive the details of their first day, the airport map, LimaTours contact 24/7 and will be able to review their complete itinerary.
masi_texto_3: During the trip, they will receive a daily mail with the itinerary and pick-up times for the next day.
masi_texto_4: Within 24 hours after departure, they will receive an email thanking them for their visit with a survey to evaluate our services. Results can be shared with the international travel agency.
masi_texto_5: Email messages can be customized with your company logo.
masi_texto_6: Additionally, they will be able to interact with our chatbot to resolve any doubts through WhatsApp.
iniciativa_verde_title: HELP CARE FOR THE PLANET WITH LIMATOURS
iniciativa_verde_texto_1: Tourists are increasingly determined to make a positive contribution to the places they visit and are more and more aware of global warming.
iniciativa_verde_texto_2: The rich natural diversity of Peru is on of its greatest attractions, enchanting thousands of tourists each year. Nevertheless, climate change and pollution are significant threats. Lima Tours enables you to help conserve the forest and so help to slow down global warming.
iniciativa_verde_texto_3: If you book a tour with us we will calculate the travel footprint of your travellers and give you a choice of carbon offsets depending on where the group flies from. We do this through a reforestation project, replanting native tree species in degraded forest of Tambopata in Madre de Dios, known as one of the world's most important biodiversity hotspots.
iniciativa_verde_texto_4: Make your travellers' holidays become a unique and positive contribution towards caring for the planet.
iniciativa_verde_punto_1: USD 6 - Activities in Peru and international flights within Latin America.
iniciativa_verde_punto_2: USD 9 - Activities in Peru and international flights from the United States.
iniciativa_verde_punto_3: USD 12 - Activities in Peru and international flights from Europe.

condiciones_tercera_personas_nino_text_3: CHD from 6 to 11 years is considered CHD rate with bed

additional_extras_text_1: A wide range of excursions and optional pre and post-program extensions are available. If you have any additional questions, please write to
prevention_protocols_covid_19_text_1: In recent months the institutions in charge of archaeological sites, museums, hotels, tour operators and the government have been preparing for the restart of tourism operations. After several months, the country is reopening its doors to the world to provide tourism services in a safe and coordinated way.
prevention_protocols_covid_19_text_2: Below we present the protocols that LimaTours complies with for the safety of your passengers.
carbon_footprint_compensation_text_1: What can we do to enjoy our Peru Trip while protecting our fragile ecosystems?
carbon_footprint_compensation_text_2: LimaTours, together with the NGO Green Initiative, offers you the option of mitigating the carbon footprint of your trip through two complementary actions:
carbon_footprint_compensation_text_3: Acquisition of UN-certified Carbon Reduction Emissions of 1 ton of carbon dioxide (CO2), corresponding to the footprint of your Peru trip.
carbon_footprint_compensation_text_4: Plantation of 1 native tree in Madre de Dios, one of the areas with the greatest biodiversity on the planet, through the ForestFriends initiative.
carbon_footprint_compensation_text_5: You will be able to download a digital certificate attesting your collaboration. This initiative seeks to engage travellers in Peru in a climate positive travel experience and plant, with their support, 1 million native trees by December 2030.
carbon_footprint_compensation_text_6: To learn more about the project and get involved in biodiversity conservation and carbon-neutral travel, visit
carbon_footprint_compensation_text_7: Thank you for contributing to the care of the planet with LimaTours!
general_recommendations_text_1: We recommend comfortable and light clothing for visiting the coastal areas of Peru and warm clothes for the high Andean area. Visitors are not advised to wear sleeves shirts, shorts, short skirts or short clothing when visiting religious temples or official buildings. Sunscreen, sunglasses, hat and mosquito repellent can be very useful during your stay.
general_recommendations_text_2: To learn more about our recommendations click
title_recommendations_ambient: RECOMMENDATIONS FOR ENVIRONMENTALLY AND CULTURALLY RESPONSIBLE TRAVEL
recommendations_ambient_list1: Do not feed or buy wild animals, always try to keep your distance.
recommendations_ambient_list2: Avoid buying endemic plants and products derived from endangered species.
recommendations_ambient_list3: Minimize water use; do not leave faucets or showers running.
recommendations_ambient_list4: Reduce energy use: turn off lights, air conditioning, and unplug your charger when not in use.
recommendations_ambient_list5: Reduce plastic use: Choose refillable water and recycle your disposable bottles.
recommendations_ambient_list6: Avoid the use of sprays, including repellents, suntan lotions, sunscreens, etc.
recommendations_ambient_list7: Ask permission before taking someone's picture.
recommendations_ambient_list8: Do not include minors in the photos.
recommendations_ambient_list10: Respect the indications for the preservation of the material heritage.
recommendations_for_luggage_transfer_ite: The passenger may take up to 20 kg of checked luggage and 5 kg in a hand luggage. The excess will be allowed according to the capacity of the bus and will have an additional cost.
recommendations_for_luggage_transfer_tit: ON BOARD THE TRAIN TO MACHU PICCHU
recommendations_for_luggage_transfer_ite: Carry the same document that you presented to the agency, given that train tickets and entrance to Machu Picchu will be issued with this information.

In all train services to Machu Picchu, the passenger may carry only hand luggage (backpack, bag or briefcase) of no more than 5 kg/ 11 lb on the train to/from Machu Picchu. You will find your luggage at the chosen hotel.
Allowed size: 62 linear inches 157cm (height + length + width)

During the stay in the lodges of Puerto Maldonado, only medium luggage is permitted. The maximum luggage weight allowed in Inkaterra and Hacienda Concepción is 10 kg, and in Refugio or Posada Amazonas is 15 kg. During your stay, your largest luggage will be stored in the hotel’s offices in Puerto Maldonado.

LimaTours acts as an intermediary for the reservation of services, so that it can’t be held responsible for the quality or suitability of services, death, injury, illness, damage, accident, loss, theft, or loss of good, delay or any other irregularity arising directly or indirectly from the provision of services by suppliers.
